feedingProfile.preferredNpk: low-N / high-K once fruiting (e.g. 8-32-16 to start, high-potassium tomato feed when fruits swell)

commonIssues: Blossom-end rot from inconsistent watering and calcium uptake; keep soil evenly moist
Flower drop when night temperatures fall below ~13C (55F) or fruit set fails above ~32-35C (90F)
Early blight, Septoria leaf spot and late blight in wet conditions
Top-heavy plants blow over in undersized containers; stake or cage at planting
Container plants dry out fast and may need daily watering in peak summer heat

source.citation: Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC - Tomatoes in Containers (container size 14-20 in / 5-gallon buckets, min 6 h full sun, daily watering in summer, balanced fertilizer weekly, cage/stake cultivars over 2 ft)
Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC - Tomatoes in Containers (minimum 6 hours full sun; daily watering needed in peak summer)

University of Minnesota Extension - Growing tomatoes in home gardens (1 inch water per week, soak at base to avoid blossom-end rot; full sun; frost-sensitive, harvest before first frost; stake/cage at planting; fertilize when first fruits enlarge; common issues: blossom-end rot, cracking, sunscald, early blight, Septoria)
University of Minnesota Extension - Growing tomatoes in home gardens (planting after frost danger, mid-April seed start in MN; blossom-end rot, cracking, blights)

University of Missouri Extension G6461 - Growing Home Garden Tomatoes (optimal ~75F; growth impaired below 50F, flower drop with night temps below 55F; 1-2 in water/week; low-N high-P medium-high-K starter 8-32-16/6-24-24 then side-dress; support recommended; Missouri planting windows late April-May)
University of Missouri Extension G6461 - Growing Home Garden Tomatoes (fertilizer low N, high P, med-high K; side-dress when first fruits one-third grown and after first harvest)
University of Missouri Extension G6461 - Growing Home Garden Tomatoes (1 to 2 inches of water per week via rainfall or irrigation)

Royal Horticultural Society - Tomatoes / grow your own (plant out when reliably above 16C/60F; single plant in 30-45cm pot; feed every 10-14 days with high-potassium liquid feed once fruits swell; keep evenly moist to prevent splitting; support cordons; blossom-end rot, splitting, blight)
Royal Horticultural Society - Tomatoes / grow your own (30-45cm / 12-18in pot per single plant; container plants need daily watering in hot weather)

The Old Farmer's Almanac - Growing Tomatoes in Containers (at least 6-8 hours direct sun; surrounding temperature ~70F+; move pots out when night temps reliably above 55F; 5-gal minimum, indeterminate 10+ gal; one plant per container)
The Old Farmer's Almanac - Growing Tomatoes in Containers (cherry 2-3 gal, determinate 5 gal minimum, indeterminate 10+ gal; containers at least 12 in deep)

Estimated values not directly stated by a single source: hotWeatherMultiplier (2x) derived from extension guidance that container tomatoes need daily watering in peak summer vs a ~2-day base interval; rainSkipThresholdMm (~13mm ~= 0.5 in) and overwaterRisk medium (water-loving but rot-prone in poorly drained pots) are conservative app-engine estimates; heatStressC 32C from the ~90F poor-fruit-set threshold; verticalSuitability 0.6 reflects indeterminate vines train well on stakes/trellis but are heavy and not true climbers; postHarvestIntervalDays and containerLeachAdjustment are engine smoothing estimates from extension side-dressing/weekly-feed guidance.
